findings.summary: Unannotated components collapsed: total_components=2 but 7 actual components present
findings.details: filterbank.sch contains 3 inductors (L?) and 4 capacitors (C?), all unannotated with '?' suffix. The statistics.total_components reports 2 and unique_parts reports 2, collapsing all instances down to one per reference stem. The components array correctly lists all 7 entries, creating an internal inconsistency. The BOM also shows quantity=1 for L and quantity=1 for C instead of the actual counts. This is a de-duplication bug where unannotated references sharing the same stem (e.g., all 'L?') are merged rather than counted individually.

findings.summary: Three-band LC low-pass filter bank not detected as lc_filters
findings.details: wspr_tx_v1.sch contains three separate 3-pole LC low-pass filter chains (9 inductors + 12 capacitors labeled L1-L9 and C4-C15), used for harmonic filtering of WSPR RF output. The signal_analysis.lc_filters array is empty despite the clear LC ladder topology. Similarly, rf_chains is empty even though SMA connectors J2/J3/J4 provide the RF output interface. The crystal oscillator circuit (Y1 + load caps) was also not detected. This is a significant missed detection for an RF design.

findings.summary: LC low-pass filter bank (6 inductors, 8 capacitors) not detected in lpf_filters sub-sheet
findings.details: lpf_filters.sch is a dedicated low-pass filter bank sub-sheet with 6 inductors (L1-L6) and 8 capacitors (C9-C16). The component count is correctly reported (total_components=14), but signal_analysis.lc_filters remains empty. This is a two-band LC ladder filter network for WSPR transmitter harmonic suppression. The detector does not recognize the LC ladder topology formed by series inductors with shunt capacitors to GND.

findings.summary: kicad_version reported as 'unknown' for KiCad 5 PCB file (file_version=20171130)
findings.details: All three KiCad 5 PCB files in kicad_wspr (wspr_tx_v1, wspr_tx_m0_v1, si5351_featherwing_v1) report kicad_version='unknown' despite having file_version=20171130 which is the KiCad 5.x format. The same issue appears in kicad_templates where all 15 KiCad 5 boards show 'unknown'. By contrast, the schematic analyzer correctly reports '5 (legacy)' for the matching .sch files. The PCB analyzer is missing a mapping from file_version=20171130 to 'KiCad 5' or similar.
